stories.title: World Trade Tensions Rise as New Tariffs Take Effect
stories.summary: Many countries are responding to trade tariffs introduced by the United States, leading to big discussions about how countries buy and sell goods to each other. Leaders around the world are meeting to figure out how to keep trade fair and friendly. This could affect the prices of things we buy every day.
stories.videoQuery: world trade tariffs explained for kids 2026

stories.title: India Celebrates New Financial Year Begins
stories.summary: April 1st marked the start of a brand new financial year in India, which means the government's new budget plans and tax rules officially kicked in. Businesses and families across India are looking at what changes the new year brings to their money matters. It is an important time for planning and saving.

stories.title: Scientists Track Early Monsoon Signals Over Indian Ocean
stories.summary: Weather scientists are closely watching the Indian Ocean for early signs of how the 2026 monsoon season might behave. Good monsoon rains are very important for farmers in India who need water for their crops. Forecasters say the early signals look interesting and they will share more updates soon.

infographic.fact1: Countries buy goods from each other in a process called 'imports' and sell goods to other countries called 'exports'.
infographic.fact2: A tariff is a special tax that a country charges on goods coming in from another country.
infographic.fact3: The World Trade Organization, or WTO, helps countries settle disagreements about trade rules.
infographic.fact4: India is one of the world's top exporters of rice, spices, and software services.

quiz.q: What do we call the tax a country puts on goods it buys from another country?
When does India's new financial year begin every year?
Which ocean do scientists watch closely to predict India's monsoon season?
